content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Eliminated the duplicate journal detail structs by moving the canonical owned types (`StartJournalDetails`, `CompletionJournalDetails`) into model.rs with `Serialize + Deserialize + Clone + deny_unknown_fields`, and removing the duplicate definitions from fs.rs. Both the render path (model.rs `render_*` functions) and the parse path (fs.rs `parse()` via `serde_json::from_str`) now use the exact same struct definition, making field-set drift structurally impossible — adding a field to the render function requires updating the struct, which automatically updates the parse path. The private borrowed `StartJournalDetailsPayload<'a>` and `CompletionJournalDetailsPayload<'a>` types were removed since the render functions now construct and serialize the owned types directly. The fs.rs `impl` blocks for `parse()`, `merge()`, and `render()` remain as inherent impls on the model types (valid within the same crate).

## Steps

1. [Completed] Added public owned StartJournalDetails and CompletionJournalDetails structs to model.rs with Serialize + Deserialize + Clone + deny_unknown_fields. These are the single canonical definition of the journal detail field sets
2. [Completed] Updated render_start_journal_details and render_completion_journal_details in model.rs to construct and serialize the new owned types instead of the private borrowed payload types. Removed the now-unnecessary StartJournalDetailsPayload<'a> and CompletionJournalDetailsPayload<'a> private structs
3. [Completed] Removed duplicate StartJournalDetails and CompletionJournalDetails struct definitions from fs.rs. Added imports of the model types. Kept the impl blocks for parse(), merge(), render() as same-crate inherent impls on the model types
4. [Completed] Updated round-trip test doc comments in fs.rs to reflect that both render and parse now use the same canonical struct from model.rs, making drift structurally impossible rather than test-detectable
5. [Completed] Ran cargo test (1163 total: 351 lib + 128 integration + 1 doc + 683 unit, 0 failed), cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check on modified files (clean)

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 1163 total tests passed (351 lib + 128 integration + 1 doc + 683 unit), 0 failed
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
- cargo fmt --check: fs.rs and model.rs clean; only pre-existing formatting diffs in unrelated tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs
- Structural guarantee: render_start_journal_details constructs StartJournalDetails (model.rs:371), and StartJournalDetails::parse deserializes into the same type (fs.rs:2280-2281). Adding a field to the render function requires adding it to the struct, which automatically makes the parse path handle it — drift is a compile error, not a test failure
- deny_unknown_fields on both types provides runtime defense-in-depth: if any code path were to somehow produce JSON with extra fields, deserialization would fail immediately rather than silently discarding them
- Existing round-trip tests (start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render, completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render) continue to pass and now verify the single canonical type end-to-end
- All model.rs tests (completion_journal_details_support_delimited_identifiers, start_journal_details_support_delimited_identifiers) continue to pass — render function behavior unchanged
- All service-level lineage tests from prior rounds (plan_hash_auto_populated_from_snapshot, terminal_replay_after_plan_evolution_succeeds, legacy_open_row_without_plan_hash_not_mislabeled_after_plan_evolution, etc.) continue to pass
- fs.rs impl blocks for parse(), merge(), render() compile as same-crate inherent impls on the model.rs types — no orphan rule violation since both the type definition and the impl are in the same crate

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing formatting drift in tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs is unrelated to these changes but will appear in cargo fmt --check across the full workspace.
